---
title: "Rscience - Parameterized Report"
format:
  html:
    output-file: "RUNNER.html"
    theme: cosmo
---    

```{r eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Libraries
  library("webshot2")
  library("chromote")
  library("knitr")
  library("plotly")
  library("htmlwidgets")
  library("webshot2")
  library("zip")

source(file = "../../AAA_fn/fn_actions.R")
source(file = "../../AAA_fn/fn_extract.R")
source(file = "../../AAA_fn/fn_run.R")

# Sourcing local files
source("fns.R")
```

```{r eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Settings
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.width = 6,
  fig.height = 4,
  dpi = 150,
  fig.align = "center"
)
```

```{r eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Files
  str_file_name_RData_internal <- "zzz_output_f03_RData.RData"
  str_file_name_zip <- "RUNNER.zip"


# Folders
  str_input_folder_path <- "../../zzz_zzz_output"
  str_html_folder <- "html_folder"
  str_png_folder  <- "png_folder"
  
# Paths
  str_file_path_RData_internal <- file.path(str_input_folder_path, str_file_name_RData_internal)
  str_file_path_zip <- file.path(str_input_folder_path, str_file_name_zip)


# Loading objects
  temp_env <- new.env()
  load(str_file_path_RData_internal, envir = temp_env)
  list_OBJ <- as.list(temp_env)
  rm(temp_env)

# Creating and cleaning folders
  fn_check_and_create_and_clean_single_folder(str_folder_path = str_html_folder)
    fn_check_and_create_and_clean_single_folder(str_folder_path = str_png_folder)

    
  # clean_and_create_folder(folder_path = str_html_folder)
  # clean_and_create_folder(folder_path = str_png_folder)
  

# Deleting files from path
  base::unlink(str_file_name_zip, recursive = TRUE)
  
```





```{r eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

vector_names_plots <- grep("^plot", names(list_OBJ), value = TRUE)
list_plots <- list_OBJ[vector_names_plots]

```

```{r eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# 1. Bucle para guardar los objetos ggplot como PNG
for(k in 1:length(list_plots)) {
  
  # Obtener el nombre y el objeto
  str_file_name <- names(list_plots)[k]
  selected_ggplot_obj <- list_plots[[str_file_name]]
  
  # Definir la ruta del PNG
  str_png_file  <- paste0(str_file_name, ".png")
  str_png_path  <- base::file.path(str_png_folder, str_png_file)
  
  # 2. Guardar directamente a PNG usando ggsave
  # Nota: Ajusta width, height y dpi según necesites para el Excel
  ggplot2::ggsave(
    filename = str_png_path,
    plot = selected_ggplot_obj,
    device = "png",
    width = 8,      # Pulgadas
    height = 5,     # Pulgadas
    dpi = 300,      # Alta resolución para que no se vea pixelado en Excel
    bg = "white"    # Fondo blanco (algunos temas de ggplot son transparentes por defecto)
  )
}

# 
# # 2. Guardar el objeto plotly como un archivo HTML temporal
# for(k in 1:length(list_plots)) {
#   
#   str_file_name <- names(list_plots)[k]
#   selected_plotly_obj <- list_plots[[str_file_name]]
#   
#   str_html_file <- paste0(str_file_name, ".html")
#   str_png_file  <- paste0(str_file_name, ".png")
#   
#   str_html_path <- base::file.path(str_html_folder, str_html_file)
#   str_png_path  <- base::file.path(str_png_folder,  str_png_file)
#   
#   # Modificación del objeto con plotly::config
#   selected_plotly_obj_mod <- plotly::config(selected_plotly_obj, displayModeBar = FALSE)
#   
#   # Guardar como HTML
#   htmlwidgets::saveWidget(
#     widget = selected_plotly_obj_mod, 
#     file = str_html_file, 
#     selfcontained = TRUE
#   )
#   
#   base::file.rename(from = str_html_file, to = str_html_path)
#   
#   # 3. Usar webshot2 para convertir ese HTML en un archivo PNG
#   webshot2::webshot(
#     url = str_html_path, 
#     file = str_png_path, 
#     delay = 2
#   )
# }

```

```{r eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

 
# 3. Zip file 
zip::zipr(
  zipfile = str_file_name_zip,
    files   = c(str_png_folder)

  # files   = c(str_html_folder, str_png_folder)
)


```








```{r}
#| label: integrity-check
#| echo: false
#| results: asis

# 1. Helper function for folders
get_folder_info <- function(path) {
  exists <- base::dir.exists(path)
  count  <- if(exists) length(base::list.files(path, all.files = FALSE)) else 0
  status <- if(exists) "✅ Exists" else "❌ Missing"
  return(list(status = status, count = count))
}

# 2. Helper function for files (ZIP)
get_file_info <- function(path) {
  exists <- base::file.exists(path)
  status <- if(exists) "✅ Exists"  else " Missing"
  return(status)
}

# 3. Gathering data
html_data <- get_folder_info(str_html_folder)
png_data  <- get_folder_info(str_png_folder)
zip_data  <- get_file_info(str_file_name_zip)

# 4. Building the Summary Table
summary_df <- base::data.frame(
  "Resource Type" = c("HTML Storage", "PNG Storage", "Final Output"),
  "System Path"   = c(str_html_folder, str_png_folder, str_file_name_zip),
  "Current Status" = c(html_data$status, png_data$status, zip_data),
  "File Count"    = c(html_data$count, png_data$count, "N/A"),
  check.names     = FALSE
)

# 5. Rendering the table
knitr::kable(
  summary_df, 
  caption = "File System Integrity Summary",
  align   = "llcc",
  format  = "markdown"
)


```
